title: AI Automation Engineer — Build Lead & Deal Pipeline for Commercial Real Estate Brokerage
description: We're a commercial real estate brokerage based in Southern California (8 agents, ~$2M annual GCI) looking to modernize how we operate. Right now our team is drowning in manual work — lead intake from multiple sources, follow-up sequences built in a half-configured HubSpot, deal documents scattered across email and Dropbox, and our two admins spending 60%+ of their time on data entry that should be automated. We think we need a mix of AI agents and workflow automation, but we're open to how it gets built. title: Senior AI Engineer: Automated Architectural Estimator & Plan Analysis
description: 1. Company Context Tar River Timber Works is a premium manufacturer of custom timber accents (braces, brackets, corbels) and heavy timber structures. We are disrupting the manual estimation process by building an AI-powered Spatial Commerce engine. We want to move from a builder's PDF blueprint to a signed contract and high-fidelity render in under 60 seconds. 2. The 21-Day Prototype Mission We need a functional Proof of Concept (PoC) to present to our board. This prototype must demonstrate: Plan Analysis: Ingesting a PDF blueprint and identifying locations for timber accents (gables, porches, etc.). Product Mapping: Recommending specific items from Tar River's product catalog based on detected geometry and roof pitch--or pricing a custom piece as drawn in the plans and recommending Tar River's standard options as optional cost savings options. Structural Guardrails: Automatically enforcing material safety (e.g., swapping Douglas Fir for Western Red Cedar on load-bearing spans) based on internal span tables. Instant Output: Generating a line-item quote (Shopify-integrated) and a high-fidelity 2D elevation render showing the products "installed" on the home. 3. Key Responsibilities PDF Vision Pipeline: Develop a system using Multimodal LLMs (e.g., Gemini 1.5 Pro) to extract architectural specs from non-searchable blueprints. Logic Engine: Build a "Structural Guardrail" layer using LangGraph or Python to validate AI recommendations against physics/safety data. Automated ArchViz: Create a "Headless" rendering flow (using Blender API or Generative AI/ControlNet) to overlay timber products onto 2D plan elevations. Shopify Integration: Connect the engine to the Shopify Storefront API for real-time pricing and cart creation. 4. Required Technical Skillset AI/Vision: Expert in Vision-Language Models (VLM), RAG, and Document AI (PDF data extraction). Logic: Proficiency in LangChain or LangGraph for multi-step reasoning and guardrails. Rendering: Experience with automated ArchViz (e.g., Stable Diffusion/ControlNet, Blender Python, or Adobe Firefly APIs). Full-Stack: Strong Python (FastAPI/Django) and Next.js experience. Commerce: Proven experience with Headless Shopify or the Storefront API. -- Board MVP Deliverable Summary Project Title: Tar River Timber Works "Instant Estimator" This prototype demonstrates how AI can serve as a Technical Sales Engineer, instantly converting builder plans into revenue while enforcing your structural and pricing standards. The 4 "Mic-Drop" Moments for the Board: Automated "As-Drawn" Estimation: The AI reads the builder's PDF and calculates a quote for exactly what is drawn. It automatically applies a 55% gross margin and adds the Custom Design & Templating Fee. Standardized Alternatives: The AI identifies opportunities where a Standard Tar River Accent (pre-engineered) can replace a custom design. It presents these as "Standard Product Options" with more aggressive pricing and no design fee. The Structural Guardrail (Safety Audit): Action: If the plan draws a structural member in Cedar, the AI overwrites the spec to Douglas Fir. Note to Board: This demonstrates how the AI protects the company’s liability by ensuring every quote is structurally sound before a human even touches it. High-Fidelity Elevation Render: The AI generates a 2D "After" photo of the home elevation, showing the quoted timber products with realistic textures and lighting.

title: Full-Stack Developer — AI-Powered Content Automation SaaS (Node.js, Next.js, Claude API, BullMQ)
description: IMPORTANT: Do not contact me outside of Upwork. I work exclusively through the Upwork platform. Any contact via LinkedIn, email, or any other channel will be disqualified. OVERVIEW We're building a content automation platform that generates bespoke social media content in each client's unique voice, runs it through a campaign strategy engine, and publishes it automatically on a schedule. This is not a simple CRUD app. It is a scheduling system, a messaging system, and a publishing system that happens to produce content. We need a developer who treats reliability and observability as first-class requirements, not afterthoughts. We have a complete product specification and developer build proposal covering data models, API contracts, queue architecture, user stories with acceptance criteria, and full edge case handling. You are not being asked to design the system. You are being asked to build it precisely and well. WHAT YOU'LL BUILD (PHASE 1) Voice profile engine. Structured client voice built from website scraping, social post scanning, and onboarding survey. Stored as a versioned text file. Monitored for drift over time. Campaign and content generation pipeline. Monthly campaign objects, 4-week arc enforcement, posting strategy ratios (Jab Jab Punch / 4-1-1), memory rules, hook type rotation, semantic similarity deduplication via pgvector. AI image generation. Style preset system, content moderation, 5-option image slideshow, pluggable provider interface. Approval system. Email magic link approve/decline with no login required, inline decline reason tags, 72-hour auto-post with guard checks, first-post explicit client confirmation. Publishing layer. Abstracted publish() interface over a relay API, idempotent publish worker with external_id checking, blame-assigned failure logging, exponential backoff retry. Queue system. BullMQ + Redis, 4 job types (generate, schedule, publish, notify), single worker per type at launch, designed to scale by config not code change. Pipeline inspector. Internal admin screen showing full per-post stage timeline including voice version, campaign object, prompt inputs, generated output, approval state, and publish result. Weekly digest email. Performance trend, top post, campaign progress, UTM-tracked click data. Stripe billing. $50 and $199 plans, 14-day free trial, self-serve signup. Phase 2 (blog, content clusters, WordPress publishing, email newsletter) and Phase 3 (Agency reviewer dashboard) will follow for the right developer. TECH STACK Frontend: Next.js (App Router), Tailwind CSS Backend: Node.js, tRPC or REST Database: PostgreSQL with pgvector (Supabase or Railway) Queue: BullMQ + Redis LLM: Anthropic Claude API — primary generation and classification model Email: Postmark Auth: Clerk or NextAuth Billing: Stripe Storage: S3-compatible (Supabase Storage or Cloudflare R2) Image generation: TBC — provider is abstracted and confirmed at project start Publishing relay: TBC — must hold LinkedIn Marketing Developer Platform approval Monitoring: Sentry YOU ARE THE RIGHT DEVELOPER IF You have built queue-based systems before and understand what idempotency means in a publish context — not just the concept but the implementation. You are comfortable working directly with the Anthropic Claude API including structured outputs, multi-turn prompts, confidence scoring, and embedding-based similarity checks. You use Cursor or a similar AI-assisted development environment as your primary workflow. This project is designed to be built with AI tooling and we expect it. You have handled LinkedIn OAuth and/or X OAuth including token refresh, permission scopes, and the edge cases that come with them. You write observable code. Every stage of a pipeline stores its output, failures are logged with context, and a non-technical person can understand what went wrong without reading logs. You can read a detailed spec and implement it faithfully without adding features that weren't asked for. You have shipped a SaaS product end-to-end, not just contributed to one. YOU ARE NOT THE RIGHT DEVELOPER IF You need the architecture designed for you. You treat logging and observability as things you will add later. You have not used the Claude API or a comparable LLM API in a production context. You are not comfortable with queue systems and async job architecture. You underestimate LinkedIn's OAuth quirks. ENGAGEMENT Phase 1 is fixed price or milestone-based, scoped to the build proposal. A detailed developer build proposal covering data models, API contracts, 14 user stories with acceptance criteria, and guardrails will be shared after initial screening. Expected Phase 1 timeline is 5 to 6 weeks at full-time pace using AI-assisted development tooling. Phases 2 and 3 are available for the right developer after Phase 1 delivery. TO APPLY Answer the following questions in your proposal. Applications without these answers will not be reviewed. Describe a queue-based system you have built. What job types did you use and how did you handle idempotency? Have you built with the Anthropic Claude API or OpenAI API in production? Describe what you built and what the hardest part was. Have you integrated LinkedIn OAuth in a production app? What edge cases did you run into? What AI-assisted development tools do you use day-to-day and how do they fit into your workflow? What is your honest Phase 1 estimate given the scope described above? BUDGET We are looking for a senior developer or small team. We are not looking for the cheapest option — we are looking for the right one. Include your rate and Phase 1 estimate in your proposal. Lowball estimates are a red flag, not a positive signal.

title: AI Automation Expert with Marketing background to build End-to-End Ad Creative “Content Machine”.
description: WHO WE ARE: We are a transformational advertising, performance marketing, creative, and branding agency. We fully replace large in-house marketing teams for our clients, working directly under their CEOs across every marketing function — paid media, creative production, social media, funnels, email, content, and brand. We operate on the most advanced AI tech stack in the industry — every premium generative AI subscription on the market — and we're looking for someone who can run with it. Our founding team has led hundreds of millions in annual media budgets globally for the largest ad agencies and tech companies. We launched mid-2025, currently serve 6 active clients, and are scaling aggressively toward six-figure monthly revenue. PROJECT OVERVIEW: Currently, we leverage mutliple AI sources, but it requires to perform multiple steps to turn raw files into a video with the right tone one of voice, cutting it into smaller pieces, hiring an editor, levaraging tools such as Runway and then applying multiple corrections, effectively wasting a lot of time and needing a lot of human intervention still. We need orchestration layer to create a repeatable system that turns raw brand assets into channel-ready creative kits with minimal human input and/or friction. We are looking for a highly specialized AI Expert to design and implement a custom Content Machine: an orchestration layer that turns raw brand assets into channel-ready creative kits with dramatically less manual work, fewer handoffs, and far more consistency. The current workflow uses multiple AI tools and manual interventions to go from source materials to usable advertising assets. That means too many disconnected steps: organizing raw files, prompting multiple tools, generating rough outputs, fixing tone-of-voice inconsistencies, manually editing videos, resizing creative for channels, coordinating review rounds, and managing approvals asset by assets. The goal is not to buy another isolated tool. The goal is to build a repeatable system that connects the right tools, enforces brand rules, reduces creative operations overhead, and helps the agency produce Ad Kits 10x faster while targeting a 3x efficiency improvement across the creative production workflow. PROJECT SCOPE: Design and build a Content Machine that can orchestrate a full creative workflow from intake to output. At minimum, the system should be able to: • Ingest raw files such as product shots, brand books, scripts, testimonials, UGC, founder videos, voice references, past ad winners, and campaign briefs, then generate derivative creative assets from that source. • Create a system that produces outputs for multiple channels from one source set, including Meta, Google Display, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, LinkedIn, and HTML5 banner variants without requiring manual rework for every version. • Generate both static banners and video ads from the same workflow, rather than maintaining separate production pipelines for each format. • Reduce manual friction between content strategy, generation, editing, adaptation, review, and final deliverable.: This is not a pure software engineering assignment and not a generic no-code automation task. The ideal expert understands how paid social and performance creative actually gets produced, versioned, reviewed, approved, and deployed across Meta, Google Display, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, LinkedIn, and HTML5 banner environments. IDEAL TOOLS & SKILLS PROFICIENCY: • AI workflow orchestration • Creative automation at scale (ideally for agencies or performance teams) • Prompt engineering for repeatable outputs • API-based automation and workflow design • Marketing operations • Multi-format ad production • Video generation and editing workflows • Asset taxonomy and metadata design • Template systems for brand-safe content generation • Performance marketing creative understanding WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE: • We can upload a source pack once and trigger a structured creative generation process with minimal setup friction. • The system can output multi-channel assets without requiring a human to resize, rename, and repackage everything manually. • Tone of voice, visual consistency, and brand rules hold together across variants because prompt libraries, templates, and governance are built into the workflow. • Videos can be beautifully and realistically created and cut into shorter variants and adapted for channel-native use with fewer manual editor hours. • Approval is faster because reviewers are approving systems, templates, and exceptions, not every single asset one by one. • Campaign launch readiness improves because outputs are better structured for downstream media buying and deployment. • The team spends more time on strategy, testing, and iteration, and less time acting as glue between disconnected. SUGGESTED PHASES: Applicants should propose their own methodology, but a strong answer will likely include something close to this: Phase 1: Discovery and system design Phase 2: Prototype / pilot Phase 3: Production build Phase 4: QA, documentation, and handoff PROPOSAL INSTRUCTIONS To avoid generic responses, include the following in your proposal: 1. A short explanation of similar systems built for creative ops, ad production, or AI workflow orchestration. 2. The exact stack or architecture you would consider for this project. 3. Which parts should be custom-built versus which parts should use existing platforms. 4. How you would reduce manual intervention in video and multi-format asset adaptation. 5. A realistic first milestone for the first 2 to 4 weeks. 6. Examples, screenshots, Looms, or case studies if available.

title: Claude Google Drive/Sheets MCP Integration ai automation
description: BioMatrix International is a small business with a team of three amazon ppc operators across two countries. We are looking for someone to implement it, verify it works across three machines, and hand it off with clear documentation. **We are open to your input if you have a better recommendation.** **Scope of Work** - **Google Cloud Setup.** Create project, enable Google Sheets and Drive APIs, set up a service account with the right permissions, download credentials file. - **Google Drive Folder Structure.** Create and configure the shared folder structure on Drive — knowledge base folder, prompts folder, and access controls so the right people can reach what they need. - **Claude Desktop + MCP Configuration.** Install and configure Claude Desktop with two MCP servers: one for local filesystem access and one for Google Sheets live read and write access. Config needs to be repeatable across three operator machines. - **Multi-machine Deployment.** The system needs to work on three machines in different locations — two in the US and one in Pakistan. Walk us through setup on the first machine and provide clear instructions for replicating on the others. - **Knowledge Base Folder Sync.** Shared knowledge files live on Google Drive and auto-sync to each machine via the Drive desktop app, so any operator always has the latest files without manual distribution. Credentials files must stay local only and never sync. - **End-to-End Verification.** Confirm that Claude Desktop can read a local file and connect to a live Google Sheet in the same session, with an approval gate in place so Claude does not write to the sheet until the operator explicitly approves. We will run a test session together. - **Handoff Documentation.** A short setup guide our team can follow to replicate the config on new machines, including where credentials go, what paths to update in the config file, and how to verify the connection is working. --- **Skills and Experience** - Experience setting up MCP servers with Claude Desktop or a similar AI desktop client - Google Cloud Console — service accounts, API enabling, credential management - Google Drive and Google Sheets API - Experience deploying software configurations across multiple machines in different locations - Familiarity with Python package management (uv / pip) - Clear written English for handoff documentation --- **Who We Are Looking For** The ideal candidate has already done something like this — connected an AI desktop client to external tools via a protocol layer and deployed it across multiple machines. This is a pure technical setup and documentation project. We have a detailed technical SOP describing our intended approach and are happy to share it with serious candidates. We are open to your recommendation if a different setup achieves the same outcome more reliably — the goal is a working, maintainable system, not a specific implementation.

title: Expert Google Apps Script Developer for Iterative AI Automation Build
description: I am looking for an experienced Google Apps Script developer to help me build a custom, AI-powered "Context Management System." I have already fully architected the system, mapped the data flows, and written the exact step-by-step logic. I am not looking to just hand off a prompt; I want to work iteratively with a developer to build, test, and verify this system module by module. My Available Tool Stack: The system will be deployed within my enterprise environment. I have access to: Gemini Enterprise Google AI Studio Gemini CLI Google Workspace Studio Project Overview (The "Clean Slate" System): We are building a background automation that reads incoming emails, synthesizes them against a master strategic framework using the Gemini 1.5 Pro API, and performs a simultaneous "Double-Write" action to two separate Google Docs. Technical Architecture & System Details: The system relies on four core modules. You will be responsible for writing the clean, modular code to execute these steps: The Scout (via GmailApp): An Apps Script time-driven trigger that searches for unread Gmail threads under a specific label. It must extract the sender's email and the raw text of the newest message. The Registry (via SpreadsheetApp): A lookup function that takes the extracted sender's email, searches a central Google Sheet routing table, and returns specific target Document IDs (a Context Doc and a Log Doc). The Brain (via UrlFetchApp & Gemini API): A function that sends the new email text, the client's current Context Doc text, and a static Master Framework Doc text to the Gemini API. You must configure the API call to return a strictly formatted JSON object containing two specific fields: newState and changeSummary. The Double-Write (via DocumentApp): Parsing the JSON response to perform two distinct text manipulation actions: The Overwrite: Use body.clear() and body.setText() on the Context Doc (erasing old information and writing the new AI-synthesized state). The Append: Use body.insertParagraph(0, text) on the Log Doc (prepending a timestamped summary of the update to a running history file). Working Style (Iterative Development): We will build this in isolated phases. I want you to write the code for one module (e.g., the API connection), pass it to me so I can run and test it in my environment, and then we move to the next piece. Requirements: Expertise in Google Apps Script (Code.gs) and creating time-driven triggers. Deep experience interacting with Google Workspace APIs. Experience integrating LLM APIs using UrlFetchApp, specifically managing context windows and parsing structured JSON payloads from AI responses. Comfortable building in modular phases, writing clean try/catch error handling, and communicating clearly during testing. To Apply, Please Provide: A brief summary of similar Apps Script or Workspace API automations you have built. Confirmation that you are comfortable working iteratively and testing module-by-module. Your estimated timeline to deliver the first working module (e.g., a basic Apps Script ping to the Gemini API returning a JSON response).

Current Challenges Dynamic UI behaviour in modern web apps (React / SPA frameworks) Element detection issues due to changing DOM structures Session instability and unexpected browser crashes Timing issues related to async rendering and hydration UI inconsistencies after viewport or layout changes Tech Stack Python (core pipeline) Selenium + ChromeDriver FFmpeg (screen recording) AI APIs (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI) Chrome profiles / session persistence macOS environment What You’ll Work On 1. Browser Automation Reliability Improve element detection in dynamic interfaces Replace brittle selectors with more robust strategies Implement smarter waits and state validation 2. Session & Authentication Stability Ensure consistent session handling across runs Improve login reliability and state persistence Manage cookies, profiles, and browser state effectively 3. Viewport & Rendering Stability Resolve issues caused by layout/viewport changes Ensure UI is stable before actions are executed Improve consistency for recorded sessions 4. Selenium Stability Debug and resolve browser/session crashes Improve lifecycle and error handling of the driver 5. Planner → Execution Alignment Improve how AI-generated actions map to real UI elements Reduce retries, mismatches, and failed executions What We’re NOT Looking For Basic Selenium scripts Simple scraping experience Junior developers without experience in complex web apps Ideal Candidate Strong Python + Selenium experience in real-world environments Experience working with modern frontend frameworks (React / SPA apps) Comfortable debugging complex browser and automation issues Understands how to build reliable, repeatable automation systems Bonus Experience (Nice to Have) Playwright or Puppeteer Experience with large web dashboards (e.g. ecommerce/admin tools) FFmpeg or video pipelines AI agent / LLM-driven workflows Example Problem You’ll Solve The system successfully navigates to a web dashboard, but UI changes (e.g. layout shifts or re-renders) cause elements to become unstable or unavailable. We need a robust solution to ensure actions execute reliably before recording begins. Engagement Initial: Stabilise current pipeline Ongoing: Expand to additional workflows and improve robustness Potential long-term collaboration To Apply Please include: Relevant projects (especially automation on real web apps or dashboards) Your approach to building reliable browser automation systems Preferred tools (Selenium vs Playwright vs hybrid) Any experience with AI-driven automation

title: GoHighLevel + Appointwise + n8n Specialist — AI Quoting Automation & WhatsApp Workflow Build
description: I run a marquee hire business (Hornchurch Marquees) and have an existing AI-powered lead qualification and quoting system built across GoHighLevel (GHL), Appointwise, n8n, and AppLevel (WhatsApp integration). The system currently works but has a key inefficiency I need solved: the AI delivers a preliminary quote that redirects customers to my website pricing page, which adds friction and reduces conversion. I want to fix this and add automatic media delivery. WHAT THE SYSTEM CURRENTLY DOES: - Inbound leads arrive via Instagram DM, WhatsApp (personal number via AppLevel), and Facebook instant lead forms - An Appointwise AI agent qualifies the lead (postcode, guest count, date, garden photo) - A preliminary quote is sent via WhatsApp - The current quote redirects leads to a website URL to view pricing — this needs to change WHAT I NEED BUILT: This is a three-phase project: Phase 1 — Inline Quote Delivery Replace the website redirect with a fully formatted quote sent directly in the WhatsApp conversation. The quote must be dynamically assembled based on the lead's guest count and produce two marquee size options, the standard included features, a bookable extras list, and a tables/chairs section — all in a specific format I will provide. The recommended architecture (already outlined in my technical brief) is: - Appointwise handles conversation and qualification - Once data is collected, a webhook fires to n8n - n8n looks up pricing from a master table (Airtable or Google Sheets) - n8n builds the formatted quote message and returns it to GHL/AppLevel for delivery Phase 2 — Automatic Size-Specific Photo Delivery Following the quote message, the system should automatically send 2–3 relevant setup photos based on the recommended marquee size. Images will be organised in Google Drive folders by size (e.g. /6x10m, /6x12m etc.). n8n should look up the correct folder and send the images. Phase 3 — State-Based Follow-Up Sequences Build conditional follow-up workflows in GHL based on lead status after quote delivery (e.g. no reply after 6hrs, no reply after 24hrs, question asked, handoff requested). I have a draft of the follow-up message sequence I want to use. TECH STACK IN USE: - GoHighLevel (GHL) — CRM, workflows, pipeline - Appointwise — AI agent and knowledge base - n8n (self-hosted on edbenjohnson.app.n8n.cloud) — automation and data mapping - AppLevel — WhatsApp integration (personal WhatsApp number) - Google Drive — media library - Airtable or Google Sheets — to be decided with successful candidate WHAT I AM LOOKING FOR: - Proven hands-on experience with GoHighLevel workflows (not just familiarity) - Direct experience with n8n — must be able to build multi-step flows, webhooks, conditional logic, and HTTP requests - Experience integrating Appointwise or a comparable AI agent platform with GHL - Understanding of WhatsApp API delivery via AppLevel or similar - Ability to read and understand an existing system and extend it — this is not a greenfield build - Excellent written English — I work through complex ideas verbally and in writing and I need someone who can match that level of communication precisely WHAT I AM NOT LOOKING FOR: - Developers who propose a full rebuild when the existing system just needs extending - Hourly billing that drags — this will be a fixed-price project with milestones - People who go quiet and surface only when they need more information TO APPLY, PLEASE ANSWER THESE THREE QUESTIONS IN YOUR PROPOSAL: 1. Describe a specific project where you built a webhook-driven, conditional quote or data-routing flow in n8n. What were the inputs, what logic did you build, and what was the output? 2. Have you worked with Appointwise before? If not, which comparable AI agent platform have you integrated with GHL, and how did the handoff logic work? 3. How would you approach building a deterministic quote engine in n8n that reads from a pricing table and outputs a consistently formatted message? Walk me through your approach in plain English. Proposals that do not answer all three questions will not be considered. PROJECT TYPE: Fixed price, milestone-based ESTIMATED TIMELINE: 2–3 weeks BUDGET: $700 COMMUNICATION: Must be available for a 30-minute video call as part of the screening process. Strong written and spoken English required.

title: After Effects Automation Specialist — Connect AI to After Effects

description: What We're Building We're an animation agency building a system where an AI assistant (Claude by Anthropic) can control Adobe After Effects through natural language commands. Example: A user types "Take the master 60-second video and create a vertical 15-second version for TikTok" — and After Effects automatically duplicates the composition, resizes it, repositions layers, trims the timeline, and queues the render. No manual work. The connection between Claude and After Effects uses an open protocol called MCP (Model Context Protocol). There are existing open-source GitHub repos that handle the bridge — we need someone to set it up, extend it with custom commands for our workflow, and get a working demo running. What We Need 1. Set up the bridge between Claude and After Effects on macOS There are existing open-source MCP servers for AE (links below). Install one, get the bridge panel running inside After Effects, and verify that Claude can send commands and AE executes them. 2. Write custom After Effects scripts for our production workflow We need a handful of ExtendScript commands that handle things like: duplicating a master composition at different aspect ratios, repositioning layers to fit new dimensions, trimming timelines, swapping text content, and queuing renders. These get registered as tools that the AI can call. 3. Document and demo the workflow Write up how to run it, and record a short screen capture showing the full flow working end-to-end. Required Skills Expert-level After Effects scripting (ExtendScript / JSX) Comfortable with Node.js and the command line macOS environment Bonus: experience with CEP panels, MCP, Nexrender, or any AE automation framework Deliverables Working Claude-to-AE bridge on macOS Custom ExtendScript tools for our content engine operations A sample AE project demonstrating the pipeline Setup documentation Short screen recording of the demo working Timeline & Budget Budget: Open to proposals — roughly 20–30 hours of work. Quote your rate or a fixed price. Start: Immediately. We need someone who can begin this week. Tell us: — Examples of AE scripts you've built (especially anything involving batch comp operations or render automation) — Your comfort level with Node.js and the command line — When you can start — Your timezone

title: Automated Client Onboarding Process Development Using AI
description: AI Developer – Client Onboarding Automation System (Trading SaaS) Overview We are building an automated onboarding system for a high-ticket trading software company, Vector Algorithmics. Our current onboarding process involves multiple manual steps across CRM, communication tools, and trading infrastructure. The goal is to design and implement a scalable system that automates client progression, reduces admin workload, and introduces AI-driven monitoring. We are currently migrating from GoHighLevel to Zoho and want this system built with that transition in mind. Objective Develop a structured onboarding system that: - Automates client movement through pipeline stages - Reduces manual follow-ups and administrative work - Flags issues such as delays, inactivity, or incomplete setup - Build a customer support bot that guides clients Introduces AI-based monitoring for client account performance Current Onboarding Flow Stage 1 – Setup (To be automated) Clients are required to set up: - NinjaTrader - IBKR brokerage account - VPS (QuantVPS) This is currently handled through a custom onboarding page hosted on Vercel. The goal is to streamline and track this process more effectively. An idea we have is to build a customer support bot that will help the client as they set themselves up. Stage 2 – Onboarding Call (Manual) - A live onboarding call with the client. This step will remain human-led. Stage 3 – Check-In (Partially automatable) - A follow-up approximately one week after onboarding to confirm system functionality. - We are looking to automate parts of this stage by building a system that can: - Verify the client account is operating correctly - Compare performance against a reference (master account) - Detect technical or configuration issues Key Requirements Pipeline Automation (Zoho CRM) - Trigger reminders when clients remain in a stage too long - Automatically create tasks for overdue actions - Escalate stalled clients to management - Stage Control - Prevent clients from advancing stages unless required fields and setup steps are completed - Communication Automation - Automated reminders for scheduled onboarding calls - Follow-ups if onboarding is not scheduled - Detection of no-shows with rescheduling prompts - Identification of unresponsive clients (currently using email / WhatsApp) - Setup Readiness Detection - Automatically confirm when a client is ready for onboarding based on: -Broker approval, Account funding, Credential submission AI Monitoring System Develop a system that: - Compares client trading accounts with a master account - Flags inconsistencies or performance deviations - Identifies potential technical issues - Notifies internal teams when issues are detected Dashboard Create a centralized dashboard displaying: - Client stage - Assigned team member - Time spent in each stage - Clients approaching or exceeding time thresholds (Currently tracked manually using spreadsheets and CRM data) Knowledge Base Tool Build an internal tool where: - Team members can input a question - The system returns a standardized, approved response - Information is sourced from internal documentation Customer Support Handover - Automatically notify the support team when onboarding is complete - Transition the client into the monitoring/support stage Tech Stack Current and planned tools include: Frontend: Vercel CRM: GHL & Zoho (migration in progress) Support: Zendesk (transitioning to Zoho) Communication: Email, Slack and WhatsApp Open to recommendations for automation and AI tooling (e.g., n8n, Make, custom backend, OpenAI integrations). Ideal Candidate Experience building automation systems and workflow pipelines Strong knowledge of Zoho CRM and integrations Familiarity with AI-based automation or agent systems Ability to design system architecture, not just execute isolated tasks Experience in fintech or trading systems is a plus Deliverables Fully automated onboarding workflow AI-based monitoring system for post-onboarding Dashboard and alerting system Knowledge base tool Documentation for internal use Application Instructions Please include: Relevant systems you have built Your proposed approach to this project Recommended tech stack Estimated timeline

Your experience with the **Anthropic API** specifically (not just LLMs generally) 3. Our stack is evolving from no-code-heavy workflows to a code-first, AI-agent-native architecture. We're building on Claude Code and adding a custom application for intelligent agent orchestration, with Clay remaining in the stack for enrichment and signal detection. If you're the automation-native builder who's outgrowing no-code and ready to write real code — this is the gig. What you'll actually be doing: Building and extending AI agent systems in Claude Code and Custom App — from signal detection and lead scoring to campaign orchestration and response handling Connecting outbound tools via API and webhook (Email Bison, HeyReach, Smartlead, n8n) — and increasingly building custom integrations in code when off-the-shelf connectors fall short Building signal-detection workflows that trigger outreach based on funding events, hiring signals, technographic data, and website visitor activity Writing Python or JavaScript to extend automation capabilities beyond what no-code tools can handle Troubleshooting enrichment failures, grading agent logic, and data quality issues upstream before they corrupt downstream workflows You're a fit if: You can write Python, JavaScript, or both. Maybe you started with JSON prompting and API calls, then moved into scripting — and now you're pushing into more complex systems. The trajectory matters as much as where you are today You're comfortable in a CLI and have used (or are ready to learn) AI-native development environments like Claude Code or Cursor You've built Clay waterfalls from scratch, not just cloned templates — and you can explain the data architecture behind them You can connect two tools via webhook in your sleep and debug a broken n8n flow without hand-holding You've worked with LLMs (Claude, GPT, or similar) as core automation components — not just bolted-on features. You understand structured prompt logic, chaining agent actions, and building AI-powered workflows that handle edge cases You're comfortable in a CLI and have used (or are ready to learn) AI-native development environments like Claude Code or Cursor You understand outbound GTM context — how signals become lists, lists become sequences, replies become meetings. You've built systems across that chain You've worked across multiple client accounts or campaigns simultaneously without things falling apart "I've used Clay" isn't enough — we need "I've built systems in Clay that process thousands of records reliably and I'm ready to build the next layer in code" Strong bonus points: Production coding experience — you've built, deployed, and maintained software that handles real workloads Active in Clay communities, automation forums, or GTM engineering circles — you ship publicly and stay connected to how the tooling landscape is evolving Agency or consultancy background — you've built systems for multiple clients, not just one internal use case Experience with n8n workflow orchestration at scale Familiarity with Airtable as an operational intelligence layer What this isn't: A campaign management role — you're building the systems, not writing the emails A "set up my CRM" gig — this is automation engineering, not admin Details: Ongoing contract, 20-30+ hours/week depending on workload Clear path to 40+ hours with the right person Remote/async — we care about what you ship, not hours logged You'll work directly with the CEO/Founder and the engineering team Budget for tools and learning — if you find something that makes us better, we'll get it To apply: Only Loom recordings will be considered 1. The most interesting automation system or AI agent workflow you've built — what it did, what tools you used, and what made it work 2. Your experience level with Clay (specific — how many records, what complexity, what data sources) 3. Where you are on the coding spectrum — from "I script when I need to" to "I write production software" 4. A link to your Clay profile, GitHub, portfolio, or a Loom walkthrough of something you've built No essays. Show us what you've built.

title: Lead AI Automation Engineer

You’ll act as the bridge between client needs and engineering execution — designing automation architectures using tools like Zapier, Make (Integromat), Airtable, HubSpot, GPT, and APIs — then managing a team of junior engineers to deliver high-quality systems on time. This is a long-term leadership and profit-sharing role in one of the fastest-growing AI automation agencies on Upwork. Responsibilities: Design and document technical architectures for AI and automation projects. Manage a small team of junior engineers (2–3 per project). Review, test, and QA all deliverables before client delivery. Collaborate with our Customer Success Manager and Business Development Manager on scoping and pricing. Stay up to date with AI and no-code tools to improve build efficiency. Develop and refine Room236’s technical SOPs for repeatable, scalable delivery. Requirements: Proven experience with automation tools such as Zapier, Make (Integromat), Airtable, HubSpot, or GPT APIs. Strong understanding of APIs, webhooks, and cross-platform integrations. Demonstrated ability to lead, mentor, and manage technical teams. Excellent problem-solving and documentation skills. Strong communication — able to explain complex workflows in plain English. Bonus: Experience building GPT agents, LangChain integrations, or AI-driven business automations. Compensation: 10% commission per project (after Upwork’s 10% fee). Expected to manage ~5 active projects at a time. Typical earnings range from $2,000–$2,500/month, with potential to reach $4,000–$5,000/month as project volume grows. Optional base retainer may be added for long-term collaboration. All projects are pre-sold — no client hunting or bidding required. Working at Room236: You'll lead technical delivery across diverse, cutting-edge AI and automation projects. Manage a talented engineering team under a proven project framework. Have transparent revenue sharing and leadership-based growth opportunities. Work/Life balance: 100% remote with flexible schedule and strong team culture (Create your own schedule). If you’re a systems thinker who loves designing efficient, scalable automations — and you want to lead a team shaping the future of AI-powered workflows — we’d love to hear from you.

title: AI Automation Developer – Build Real Estate Listing → Marketing
description: Project Overview I am building a simple MVP platform for real estate agents that automatically turns a property listing into a complete marketing kit. Agents will upload their MLS listing sheet (PDF) and property photos. The system will automatically generate: • Instagram Reel • TikTok video • Instagram caption • Facebook post • LinkedIn post • Email announcement • Open house email • Print-ready flyer • Property landing page • Lead capture form • QR code for the property page This project should be built using automation tools and APIs, not full custom software development. The goal is to create a working MVP quickly and cost-effectively. Required Tech Stack Automation platform Make AI content generation OpenAI API Video generation Bannerbear Database / storage Airtable Payments Stripe Landing page / website builder WordPress or Webflow Developers may suggest equivalent tools if they reduce cost or complexity. System Workflow Step 1 – Agent Submits Listing Agent fills out a submission form. They must be able to: Upload MLS listing sheet (PDF) Upload listing photos (5–20 images) Optional fields: Agent name Agent phone Agent email Brokerage name Open house date/time The MLS listing will contain most of the property information. Step 2 – AI Extracts Listing Information The system must automatically read the MLS PDF and extract the following information: Address City / State Price Bedrooms Bathrooms Square footage Property description Property features If any information is missing, the system should allow manual editing before generation. Step 3 – AI Marketing Content Generator Using the extracted listing information, generate marketing materials. Social Media Content Generate: Instagram caption Facebook post LinkedIn post Email Marketing Generate: New listing email announcement Open house email invitation Print Flyer Generator (Important Feature) The system must automatically generate a print-ready flyer for the listing. The flyer should include: Property photos Property address Price Bedrooms / Bathrooms Square footage Property description Key features Agent contact information Brokerage logo (optional) QR code linking to the property website Flyer requirements: Print-ready format Letter size 8.5 x 11 High resolution PDF Professional real estate layout. The flyer should be automatically generated and downloadable after the listing is submitted. Step 4 – Video Reel Generator Create a vertical video slideshow using the property photos. Instagram and Tic Tok Video requirements: Vertical format 9:16 Resolution 1080x1920 Length 15–30 seconds Slides should include overlays such as: Just Listed Property address Price Bedrooms / Bathrooms Key features Call to action Example: “Schedule a showing today” The output should be a downloadable MP4 file. Step 5 – Property Website Generator The system should optionally generate a simple property landing page using the listing information. The page should include: Property photos Generated reel video Property description Property details Agent contact information Map of property location Step 6 – Lead Capture System The property website must include a lead capture form. Form fields: Name Email Phone When a visitor submits the form: The lead should be saved in the database. The lead should be emailed to the listing agent. Step 7 – QR Code Generator The system should automatically generate a QR code for each property page. Agents can place this QR code on: Yard signs Flyers Open house materials When scanned, the QR code should open the property landing page. Step 8 – Delivery to Agent Once the marketing kit is generated, the agent should receive an email containing: Reel video download link Generated social media captions Email marketing content Print-ready flyer PDF Link to property website QR code image All files should also appear on a results page in the platform. Step 9 – Payment System Set up subscription billing using Stripe. Example plans: Starter – limited listings per month Pro – higher limit Unlimited – fair use The system must enforce usage limits. Admin Panel Admin should be able to view: Users Listings generated Videos created Leads captured Subscription status Timeline Expected timeline: 2–3 weeks This project should use automation tools and APIs, not full custom software development. Deliverables Working system deployed online Automation workflows built Video generation working AI prompts configured Stripe subscription system working Property page generator working Lead capture system working QR code generator working Flyer generator working Admin access Documentation explaining the system Full access to all accounts and automation workflows Important Notes Do NOT scrape Zillow, Redfin, Realtor, or MLS websites. Agents will upload their MLS listing sheet manually. MLS integrations are not required for the MVP. When Applying Please include: Examples of Make.com or Zapier automation projects you built Your approach for extracting data from MLS listings How you will generate the video reels Estimated timeline Monthly operating cost estimate Application Filter To confirm you read this job post, start your proposal with the word: automation

title: Build an AI-Powered Email Response & Automation System (Make.com / Airtable / Claude API / Front)
description: We send ~2,000 cold outreach emails per week and need a system to intelligently handle the 100–200 inbound replies this generates each week. The system classifies each reply (9 types), routes it appropriately, generates AI draft responses for human review, and learns from feedback over time. The full stack is already decided: Make.com for automation, Airtable Pro as the database, Front Professional as the unified inbox, the Claude API (Haiku + Sonnet) for AI, and Instantly as the outbound platform. A detailed 40-page technical specification is provided to shortlisted applicants covering every workflow, all database schemas, AI prompt structure, and phased delivery plan. The most technically demanding single deliverable is a custom JavaScript sidebar panel built on the Front App Framework. This panel must display live lead context, AI classification scores, thread summaries, and expose 9 action buttons that each fire Make.com webhooks — including a prominently flagged Reply All indicator. We've spec'd this component in detail but are genuinely open to better or more elegant approaches to its implementation. The same openness applies throughout the project: the specification defines what the system must do, not necessarily how — and we welcome suggestions from experienced developers on architecture, tooling, or execution where there's a smarter way to build it. The core delivery covers: webhook ingestion with idempotency, 5-layer spam filtering, AI classification and draft generation, the Front sidebar panel, IMAP mailbox management across 20 accounts, the full Airtable schema, follow-up nudge automation, post-publish broadcast, Ghost Yes detection, a feedback-driven learning engine with KB update proposals, urgent flag automation, and confidence-based auto-writes after calibration. Budget is $3,500. A second engagement covering full automation, auto-approval, and an analytics dashboard follows separately, and we are looking for a developer who can be a long-term partner. Please indicate your approach to the sidebar panel specifically in your proposal.
